role_summary: President of the San Francisco NAACP; head pastor of Third Baptist Church of San Francisco, 1976-2025.
biographical_paragraph: Amos C. Brown was born February 20, 1941 in Jackson, Mississippi [src: 1]. In 1955 he organized the NAACP's first youth council, and in 1956 he met Martin Luther King Jr. at the NAACP's national convention in San Francisco [src: 1]. In 1961 Brown was arrested alongside King at a lunch counter sit-in and joined the Freedom Riders [src: 1]. He completed his Bachelor's at Morehouse College in 1964, was one of eight students in a college seminar co-taught by Martin Luther King Jr. at Crozer Theological Seminary [src: 1], and went on to earn an M.Div. at Crozer and a D.Min. at United Theological Seminary [src: 1]. From 1976 until 2025 he served as head pastor of Third Baptist Church of San Francisco [src: 1] and is president of the San Francisco branch of the NAACP [src: 1]. His oral history is held by the Library of Congress as entry 2 of the Civil Rights History Project [src: 2].

sources.title: Amos C. Brown oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress
